pr.title: Split up un_2FC9.s into various remaining files
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large assembly-only organization PR that split the monolithic asm/melee/un_2FC9.s into multiple smaller ty and vi assembly files, with obj_files.mk updated to build the new objects. The PR body states the split was guided by sdata usage and data-section strings; vi was described as mostly comprehensive, while ty may still need further splitting.
what_changed: Removed nearly all content from asm/melee/un_2FC9.s, with 36081 deleted lines and only 1 added line reported.
Created new ty assembly files: toy.s, tydisplay.s, tyfigupon.s, and tylist.s.
Created new vi assembly files: vi.s, vi0102.s, vi0401.s, vi0402.s, vi0501.s, vi0502.s, vi0601.s, vi0801.s, vi1101.s, vi1201v1.s, vi1201v2.s, and vi1202.s.
Updated obj_files.mk with 20 added lines so the split assembly files participate in the build.
Moved a very large block beginning around .section .text 0x80305058 - 0x803124B8 into asm/melee/ty/toy.s, preserving labels such as un_80305058 and un_803053C4 in the diff excerpt.

The diff excerpt shows trophy-related assembly remaining under placeholder-style un_* names and calling functions such as Trophy_SetUnlockState, HSD_Randi, HSD_Randf, lbLang_IsSettingUS, gm_8016B498, gm_801A4310, and gmMainLib_8015CC78.
smart_moves: Used sdata references and data-section strings as evidence to split a large unknown assembly file into more coherent translation-unit-sized chunks.
Prioritized a mostly comprehensive split for the vi section, reducing future decomp search space for files named vi0102 through vi1202.
Kept unresolved ty material grouped into plausible ty files instead of forcing uncertain function-level names; the PR body explicitly notes ty may need further splitting.
Preserved assembly labels and address-oriented structure during the move, which minimizes risk for a pure file-split PR and makes later C matching work easier to verify.
Updated obj_files.mk alongside the file split, avoiding a common failure mode where newly split assembly files exist but are not linked into the build.
decomp_lessons: For monolithic unknown assembly files, sdata usage and nearby data-section strings are practical anchors for reconstructing original file boundaries.
A file split can be valuable even before functions are named or decompiled, because it gives future workers smaller, system-specific files to inspect.
When confidence differs by subsystem, document it: this PR states vi is mostly comprehensive but ty may require more subdivision.
Do not over-rename during an organizational split when evidence is incomplete; preserving un_* symbols keeps the change mechanical and reviewable.
Build metadata changes are part of assembly splitting work; obj_files.mk must track any new asm/melee subfiles.
naming_conventions: New trophy-related assembly files use the asm/melee/ty/ prefix and short subsystem-style names: toy, tydisplay, tyfigupon, and tylist.
New visual/vi files use asm/melee/vi/ and stage- or screen-like numeric suffixes such as vi0102, vi0401, vi1201v1, vi1201v2, and vi1202.
Unidentified functions and data retain placeholder names such as un_80305058, un_803053C4, un_804A284C, un_804D6EB4, and un_804DDCD0.
Local assembly labels remain address-derived .L_8030xxxx labels after the split.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Mechanical relocation of assembly into new files while retaining .include "macros.inc" and .section declarations.
Preserved original global labels and local labels to maintain assembler/linker identity after splitting.
Used sdata21 references such as un_804D6EB4 and un_804DDCD0 as clues for file grouping.
Used data-section strings, per PR body, as file-boundary evidence, though the excerpt does not show the specific strings.
Kept build ordering/link inclusion synchronized by editing obj_files.mk.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or formal reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
No reviewer-requested naming changes or matching tactics were recorded in the provided context.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile or further split asm/melee/ty/toy.s.
Find later PRs that replace asm/melee/ty/tydisplay.s, tyfigupon.s, or tylist.s with C.
Find later PRs touching asm/melee/vi/vi1201v1.s, vi1201v2.s, or vi1202.s.
Search for when un_80305058 and un_803053C4 receive descriptive names.
Search obj_files.mk history after PR 1048 for additional ty or vi object-file boundary corrections.
